question: What is the hot leg volume according to Table 5.1-1?

nuscale_ch05_0004.title: System Description
nuscale_ch05_0004.summary: The Reactor Coolant System (RCS) in the NuScale design is located inside the containment vessel and includes key components such as the reactor pressure vessel, pressurizer, and associated piping and controls. It removes heat from the reactor core to the steam generators using natural circulation, and features systems for removing noncondensible gases and maintaining pressure boundary integrity to prevent radionuclide release. The RCS interfaces with several other systems, including emergency core cooling and chemical control, to ensure safe and efficient operation.

nuscale_ch05_0006.title: System Evaluation
nuscale_ch05_0006.summary: This section describes the evaluation of the NuScale Power Plant’s system performance, focusing on optimizing steady-state parameters such as coolant temperatures, flow rates, and pressures to maximize electrical generation and maintain safety margins. Calculations using thermal hydraulic software and hand verification establish best-estimate, maximum, and minimum flow conditions, accounting for uncertainties and ensuring the natural circulation design is effective with minimal pressure losses.

nuscale_ch05_0003.title: Design Basis
nuscale_ch05_0003.summary: The Design Basis section outlines that the NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping, ensuring safe and efficient heat transfer from the reactor core to the steam generator. The RCS is designed to maintain proper coolant flow, water chemistry, and boron concentration to protect fuel integrity and support safe operation under normal and transient conditions.

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the construction of the pressurizer and its components within the NuScale reactor coolant system. It specifies the types of steel and alloys used for various parts, their protective claddings, and compliance with relevant BPVC material specifications to ensure corrosion resistance and compatibility with operating environments.

nuscale_ch05_0024.title: Pressurizer
nuscale_ch05_0024.summary: The pressurizer, integrated into the upper region of the reactor vessel and separated by a baffle plate, regulates reactor coolant system (RCS) pressure by maintaining a saturated steam-water interface. It uses heaters and spray flow to control pressure and is designed to handle operating transients without triggering reactor trips or safety system actuations, with instrumentation providing real-time pressure and level data to control systems.

nuscale_ch05_0002.title: Summary Description
nuscale_ch05_0002.summary: The NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation, eliminating the need for pumps or external piping, and is integrated within each NuScale Power Module. The RCS is designed to safely transfer heat from the reactor core, maintain proper coolant chemistry and boron concentration, and ensure fuel design limits are not exceeded during all operating conditions.

nuscale_ch05_0014.title: Reactor Vessel
nuscale_ch05_0014.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module's reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single vessel surrounded by a containment vessel. The RPV serves as a critical barrier to fission product release and provides structural support and attachment points for various reactor components, with materials and fabrication standards selected to ensure long-term integrity and compliance with ASME codes.

edges.description: Section B discusses the establishment of steady state values for system parameters, such as hot leg volume, which are then specifically listed in Table 5.1-1 referenced by the analysis question, while Section A provides general system descriptions without specifying these values.
Section B provides the general design basis and system boundaries for the RCS, which Section A further details by describing specific components and operational features within that system.

Section A discusses the evaluation and optimization of system parameters, which are established based on the design described in Section B, where the components and configuration of the reactor coolant system (including the hot leg) are defined.

Section A provides detailed material and component descriptions of the pressurizer, which is a subsystem referenced in Section B's general system description of the Reactor Coolant System (RCS) and its functions.

Section A provides references to detailed component descriptions and material specifications, while Section B discusses system-level evaluations and refers to Section 5.4 for further details, indicating that Section B references Section A for more specific information.
Section A provides detailed descriptions of specific pressurizer components and their materials, which are part of the broader reactor coolant system summarized in Section B.

Section A describes the physical configuration and function of the pressurizer as part of the reactor vessel, while Section B provides a general system-level description of the reactor coolant system (RCS) and its interaction with the pressurizer, connecting them through the mention of the PZR (pressurizer) volume and its operational role.

Section B discusses the establishment of steady state values for system parameters such as volumes, which are detailed in Table 5.1-1, while Section A describes the pressurizer component but does not provide the hot leg volume, thus Section B is the relevant source for the analysis question.

Section A describes the pressurizer as a component of the reactor vessel, while Section B provides a general overview of the reactor coolant system, including the pressurizer as one of its components, thus Section A specifies details for the general system described in Section B.

Section A provides a general overview of the reactor coolant system's components, while Section B specifies additional functional details about the system's degasification and pressure-retaining features, both contributing to the understanding of the RCS relevant to Table 5.1-1.

Section A provides a general overview of the reactor coolant system's components, while Section B discusses the evaluation of system parameters (such as volumes) to optimize performance, connecting the general system description to the specific parameter values found in Table 5.1-1.
Section A references Section B by indicating that the Reactor Vessel (RPV) is a component of the Reactor Coolant System (RCS), which is described in more detail in Section B.

Section A references Section B by noting that the reactor coolant system (RCS) is described in Section 5.1, which is where Table 5.1-1 (the source of the hot leg volume) is located and discussed in Section B.

Section A describes the reactor vessel as a component of the reactor coolant system (RCS), while Section B provides a summary of the RCS and identifies the reactor vessel as one of its components, thus Section A provides details/specifications for the general system described in Section B.

Section A describes the pressurizer as a component of the reactor vessel, while Section B provides a general overview of the reactor coolant system and its components, including the pressurizer, thus Section B gives a general context that Section A specifies in detail.

Section A describes the pressurizer as an integral part of the reactor vessel, which is the main subject of Section B, thereby specifying the relationship and physical integration between the two components.

Section B describes the reactor vessel as a component of the reactor coolant system (RCS), which is generally introduced and described in Section A, establishing that Section A's summary provides the context for the more detailed description in Section B.
